content: We can frame the original Self-Refine framework as a single-perspective iterative refinement baseline, and integrate the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from cutting-edge LLM self-reflection mechanisms to build a multi-perspective self-refinement paradigm that still retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra supervised training or reinforcement learning. This new paradigm would allow large language models to catch flaws in reasoning and code generation that a single self-feedback perspective would consistently miss, without increasing training overhead.
